sourceNote: AFM-only training transcription from Viking Air PSM 1-63-1A Revision 53, Section 3.4.2, pages 3-17 to 3-18. The memory list contains only the two actions printed in bold. Pulling the FIRE PULL handle discharges the extinguisher; there is no separate extinguisher-push action in this AFM configuration. Crew-role fields are trainer UI assignments only; they are not approved PF/PM callouts or operator SOP. Verify applicability against the aircraft-specific AFM, amendments, supplements, and operator procedures.

memory.action: FIRE BELL MUTE switch — Lift up to mute bell.
Set Maximum Power on the unaffected engine — Advance its power lever to the torque, T5, or NG limit, whichever is reached first; ensure its PROP lever is at MAX RPM (96% NP).

flow.action: Power lever, affected engine — Retard to the 10 PSI torque position, approximately zero thrust.
PROP lever, affected engine — FEATHER.
FUEL lever, affected engine — OFF.
FUEL OFF emergency shut-off switch, affected engine — OFF.
FIRE PULL handle, affected engine — Pull to discharge extinguisher.
BOOST PUMP switch, affected engine — OFF.
Trim aircraft as required. Flaps may remain retracted or be extended to 10° if necessary.
Compute and, if necessary, set Maximum Continuous Power.
GENERATOR switch of inoperative engine — OFF.
BLEED AIR switch of inoperative engine — OFF.
Check generator load on the operative engine; reduce electrical consumption as required to remain at or below 1.0 on the loadmeter.
Use fuel as necessary to remain within center-of-gravity limits for the remainder of the flight.
